translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul, bondman ofGod, and apostle of Jesus Christ according to [the] faith ofGod's elect, and knowledge of [the] truth which [is] according to piety;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in [the] hope of eternal life, whichGod, who cannot lie, promised before the ages of time,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but has manifested in its own due season his word, in [the] proclamation with which I have been entrusted, according to [the] commandment of our SaviourGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to Titus, my own child according to [the] faith common [to us]: Grace and peace fromGod [the] Father, and Christ Jesus our Saviour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause I left thee in Crete, that thou mightest go on to set right what remained [unordered], and establish elders in each city, as I had ordered thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if any one be free from all charge [against him], husband of one wife, having believing children not accused of excess or unruly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the overseer must be free from all charge [against him] asGod's steward; not headstrong, not passionate, not disorderly through wine, not a striker, not seeking gain by base means;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but hospitable, a lover of goodness, discreet, just, pious, temperate,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: clinging to the faithful word according to the doctrine taught, that he may be able both to encourage with sound teaching and refute gainsayers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there are many and disorderly vain speakers and deceivers of people's minds, specially those of [the] circumcision,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who must have their mouths stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which ought not [to be taught] for the sake of base gain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One of themselves, a prophet of their own, has said, Cretans are always liars, evil wild beasts, lazy gluttons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This testimony is true; for which cause rebuke them severely, that they may be sound in the faith,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not turning [their] minds to Jewish fables and commandments of men turning away from the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things [are] pure to the pure; but to the defiled and unbelieving nothing [is] pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They profess to knowGod, but in works deny [him], being abominable, and disobedient, and found worthless as to every good work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But do thou speak the things that become sound teaching;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the elder men be sober, grave, discreet, sound in faith, in love, in patience;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the elder women in like manner be in deportment as becoming those who have to say to sacred things, not slanderers, not enslaved to much wine, teachers of what is right;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may admonish the young women to be attached to [their] husbands, to be attached to [their] children,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: discreet, chaste, diligent in home work, good, subject to their own husbands, that the word ofGod may not be evil spoken of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The younger men in like manner exhort to be discreet:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in all things affording thyself as a pattern of good works; in teaching uncorruptedness, gravity,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a sound word, not to be condemned; that he who is opposed may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: bondmen to be subject to their own masters, to make themselves acceptable in everything; not gainsaying;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not robbing [their masters], but shewing all good fidelity, that they may adorn the teaching which [is] of our SaviourGod in all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the grace ofGod which carries with it salvation for all men has appeared,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: teaching us that, having denied impiety and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, and justly, and piously in the present course of things,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: awaiting the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our greatGod and Saviour Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all lawlessness, and purify to himself a peculiar people, zealous for good works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no one despise thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put them in mind to be subject to rulers, to authorities, to be obedient to rule, to be ready to do every good work,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, [to be] mild, shewing all meekness towards all men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we were once ourselves also without intelligence, disobedient, wandering in error, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, [and] hating one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the kindness and love to man of our SaviourGod appeared,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not on the principle of works which [have been done] in righteousness which we had done, but according to his own mercy he saved us through [the] washing of regeneration and renewal of [the] Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that, having been justified by his grace, we should become heirs according to [the] hope of eternal life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word [is] faithful, and I desire that thou insist strenuously on these things, that they who have believedGod may take care to pay diligent attention to good works. These things are good and profitable to men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But foolish questions, and genealogies, and strifes, and contentions about the law, shun; for they are unprofitable and vain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An heretical man after a first and second admonition have done with,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: knowing that such a one is perverted, and sins, being self-condemned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I shall send Artemas to thee, or Tychicus, use diligence to come to me to Nicopolis; for I have decided to winter there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zenas the lawyer and Apollos set forward diligently on their way, that nothing may be lacking to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let ours also learn to apply themselves to good works for necessary wants, that they may not be unfruitful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All with me salute thee. Salute those who love us in [the] faith. Grace [be] with you all.
